name: transportation_policy_licensing_backlog_millhaven_lib_eq
premise: The board wants one accountable decision that can be explained simply and audited later. At Millhaven Transportation Policy Program Office, a transportation policy unit is forced into a time-bound decision after 2 related incidents in the last 12 hours. The trigger is that a licensing backlog forces staff to choose between informal workarounds and halting time-sensitive services, while a vendor insists their SLA excludes the failure mode now happening. The board has pre-approved up to $1.8M in emergency spend if controls are documented and reversible. Stakeholders frame this as liberty vs equality, because the system’s defaults will decide who gets relief and who must prove they deserve an exception. Director Mina Cho receives contradictory reports: the metrics look stable, but operators describe edge cases that the system handled unpredictably. Community Liaison Mo Chen argues that any plan must include a credible appeals channel and a way to detect uneven error rates before they harden into routine.

agents.name: Director Mina Cho
agents.identity: I am Director Mina Cho, accountable for operational decisions at Millhaven Transportation Policy Program Office during this Transportation Policy incident window. My mandate is to keep services functioning while preserving auditability and public trust.
agents.goal: I want a choice that can survive adversarial review, distributes risk intentionally, and does not reward corner-cutting that will be repeated next week as 'normal operations.'

agents.name: Community Liaison Mo Chen
agents.identity: I am Community Liaison Mo Chen, an independent voice in this Transportation Policy process. I am watching for procedural gaps that become permanent after the crisis adrenaline fades.
agents.goal: I want explicit protections for those least able to recover from a wrong guess, even if that slows deployment or forces uncomfortable transparency in the short term.

choices: I will pursue a staged intervention: cap scope, require a time-boxed pilot with error-rate monitoring by subgroup, and expand only after verification, accepting slower relief if it buys legitimacy.
I will pursue an accelerated intervention across the affected footprint with daily executive review and public dashboards, accepting higher overreach risk to stop cascading failures now.
forced_choice.call_to_action: The trade space is ugly: either move fast on the intervention or build guardrails first. Director Mina Cho must commit. What does Director Mina Cho do?
